mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals strong labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high PAYEMS growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 200 indicates elevated inflationary pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and rise in housing starts (HOUST) signals supply-side constraints and demand-driven inflation, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of INDPRO decline and HOUST rise fails to predict negative returns in > 40% of cases over a 3-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60d returns in > 50% of observed monetary tightening periods despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% indicates a tight labor market that drives wage inflation and corporate margin pressure, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE falls below 5% despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising CPIAUCSL and stagnant FEDFUNDS indicates central bank inaction against inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative returns in > 40% of cases over a 3-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real sales (RSAFS) reflects strong consumer demand that drives corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in high RSAFS growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply chain capacity that reduces corporate profitability, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines despite the signal.
